title: Cross-form value fetch via _CF suffix calculate rows
description: To read a value from another form into the current form, emit a calculate row at the TOP of the survey, named with the suffix `_CF` (e.g. SEX_CF, AGE_CF, WEIGHT_CF, ICFDAT_CF, ARMCD_CF). The calculation uses the full clinicaldata XPath: `instance('clinicaldata')/ODM/ClinicalData/SubjectData/StudyEventData[@StudyEventOID='SE_<X>']/FormData[@FormOID='<SOURCE_FORM>']/ItemGroupData[@ItemGroupOID='<SOURCE_FORM>.<GROUP>']/ItemData[@ItemOID='<SOURCE_FORM>.<FIELD>']/@Value`. The row must have bind__oc_external=clinicaldata and MUST NOT have bind__oc_itemgroup (per OC-2 exception clause). The fetched value is then referenced via ${SEX_CF} (etc.) in downstream relevant/constraint/calculation expressions.
rationale: Forms often need a value captured elsewhere — demographics (sex, age) for sex-dependent fields; consent date for date-floor constraints; arm code for arm-specific logic. The _CF pattern is the canonical OpenClinica way to surface cross-form values into a form's expression evaluator. The naming convention (_CF suffix) makes these fetch rows visually distinct and easy to identify in form maintenance.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} is a _CF cross-form fetch — verify: (1) calculation matches the canonical clinicaldata XPath shape with correct SE_<X>/SOURCE_FORM/GROUP/FIELD substitutions; (2) bind__oc_itemgroup is EMPTY (cross-form lookups have no local itemgroup, per OC-2 exception); (3) row is at the TOP of the form's survey so downstream rows can reference its value; (4) downstream fields reference the value via ${${field.name}}.

effect.soft: Calculation generation requires knowledge of the source form's OID, the source group OID, and the source field OID. The canonical pre-blessed helpers and their source coordinates are: SEX_CF -> DM.SEX; AGE_CF -> DM.AGE; WEIGHT_CF -> VS.WEIGHT (or similar); ICFDAT_CF -> ICF.ICFDAT; ARMCD_CF -> EN.ARMCD; ENRLDAT_CF -> EN.ENRLDAT; BRTHDAT_CF -> DM.BRTHDAT. For custom helpers, the form/field OIDs must be looked up via the study's OID directory. Calculation-text generation is not yet structured in the DSL.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7L during Phase B.1c-final. Plan doc originally numbered this 7K — corrected in updated plan doc. Composes with OC-2 (itemgroup-mandatory) via the explicit exception in that rule for bind__oc_external=clinicaldata rows. The pre-blessed helper roster in the soft directive is the same one previously documented in the userMemories OC-7 K (CF helpers) entry; carried forward to the convention so it lives in the conventions store. A future per-helper convention bundle (one per blessed helper, with the source OID coordinates hard-coded) could promote this to structured for the seven canonical helpers.
